title: M.Sc. Biotechnology
content: M.Sc. Biotechnology program is of two years duration study program and is divided into four semesters. Biotechnology is the branch of the science that deals with the detail study of biotechnological aspect of cytology, Genetics, Genetic engineering, Plant and Animal biotechnology. This program includes collecting information of Immunology, biochemistry, Enzymology etc. It also includes about the genetic diversity of the microorganism and different techniques of diseases detection. The students will also learn about the role of biotechnology in environment, medical and pharmaceutical and Genetic engineering and gene therapy etc. Bachelor Degree in Basic Health or Allied Science with minimum 50% marks or equivalent Students having CUET Score will be given preference in merit. With a degree in Biotechnology, students can get job opportunities in various fields like healthcare organizations, forensic science laboratories, environmental organizations, higher education at institutions, food and beverages, publicly funded research organizations, pharmaceuticals and many other industries. Further students can explore opportunities to go for higher educations at national and international level (as research scholars). 01 02 03 04 Communication: Communicate effectively on scientific activities with the community and with society at large, such as, being able to comprehend and write effective reports and design documentation, make effective presentations, and give and receive clear instructions 05 06 07 08 01 The graduated young minds will be ignited to understand the microbial word and its scientific concepts through application based learning. 02 The graduates will be emphasized on applied aspects of advance biological techniques by hands-on training and to inculcate ethics and professional attitude. 03 The graduates will be trained for self-directed learning, recognizing, continuing educational needs in occupying positions in research, industries and related organization. 01 Developing deeper understanding of key concepts of basic and allied branches of biotechnology such as Plant and Animal biotechnology, immunology, microbiology, biotechnology, clinical and industrial biotechnology. 02 Equip students with analytical and technical skills related to biotechnology and allied subjects through application-based learning such as use of audio-visual aids, problem/project-based learning, guest lectures, visits to industrial and academic organizations and to practice evidence-based biotechnology.
